mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4.5%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth, boosting consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 4.5%, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances combined with increasing housing starts signal a synchronized expansion in durable goods and residential investment demand, which lifts corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.05 over any 18-month rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when both series rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: In a capacity-constrained expansion, a month-over-month increase in the industrial production index signals that firms are operating near maximum output, reducing supply bottlenecks and supporting near-term equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if INDPRO month-over-month changes show no correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns (correlation coefficient < 0.05) during periods where capacity utilization exceeds 80%.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth exceeds a 3.0% annualized pace, aggregate demand outpaces supply constraints, driving earnings revisions upward and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate exceeds 3.0% annualized
failure_condition: The signal produces a negative predictive rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns in high-growth quarters are not statistically different from baseline (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which sustains household income growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample prediction accuracy by at least 2% over a single-proxy baseline, or forward returns turn negative when PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent month-over-month increase in the consumer price index erodes real purchasing power and forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which compresses equity valuation multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if 5-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises sequentially, or if the signal's directional accuracy falls below 48% over a 2-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a rising federal funds rate combined with accelerating inflation signals that real borrowing costs are increasing faster than corporate earnings can adjust, which suppresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns with a statistical significance of p < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive when both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL rise simultaneously.
